source_material.title: Professional accessibility workflow survey dry run
source_material.body: Research context: professional pre-fieldwork dry run for an accessibility workflow survey. Target population: knowledge workers using internal workflow tooling across the United States. Target population size: 18000. Source sample size: 240. Intended synthetic panel size: 10. Segment variables: region, disability status, tenure band. Expected analyses: accessibility barrier topline, region cut, tenure cut, open-text friction themes. Constraints: synthetic evidence only, suppress slices below the minimum base, and keep target population scale separate from the synthetic panel.

expected_research_intake.research_context: Professional accessibility workflow survey dry run before human fieldwork.
expected_research_intake.target_population_summary: Knowledge workers using internal workflow tooling in the United States.
unresolved_gaps: No weighting plan was provided in the source brief.
The human sample recruitment criteria still need explicit accessibility-screening rules.

objectives: Validate accessibility-barrier wording before human fieldwork.
Check whether region and tenure cuts are reportable under minimum-base rules.
Confirm that open-text friction evidence is handled as coded themes rather than raw charts.
decision_questions: Is the questionnaire ready for human fieldwork?
Which segment cuts should be suppressed or tabled?
Which accessibility barriers need wording changes?
assumptions: Synthetic responses are exploratory scenario evidence only.
The synthetic panel must remain separate from the 18,000-person target population.
chart_plan: Use tables or suppression notes for unstable segment cuts.
Use evidence panels for open-text friction themes.

expected_question_rationales.rationale: Measures the headline accessibility workflow climate item.
Captures the main structured barrier behind accessibility friction.
Checks whether disclosure and participation behavior signals additional questionnaire risk.
Tests whether unstable segment cuts are handled honestly.
Captures the most important unresolved accessibility or workflow issue in open text.

expected_segmentation_behavior.suppression_rule: Use tables or explicit suppression notes instead of unstable claims.

expected_report_warnings: Do not treat 10 synthetic respondents as representative of the 18,000-person target population.
expected_human_fieldwork_handoff: Resolve weighting and recruitment criteria before fieldwork.
Lock the accessibility-screening rules before any human launch.
